behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is prompt/history construction logic (single local transformation). No real I/O is required, and mocking does not hide the primary bug if the test asserts the exact message list sent to the LLM client adapter input.
behaviors.test_description: Test the history-trimming function (or prompt-builder method) with 11+ messages and assert it outputs exactly the last 10 in order; include edge cases at 10 and 0. Keep LLM/network/db out of scope.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality and non-deterministic behavior. A mocked LLM can always be made on-topic and would hide real off-topic failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real LLM with a fixed evaluation rubric (or model-graded relevance check) over multiple trials; fail if relevance/on-topic rate falls below threshold for representative support questions.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The detection claim ('too formal') is model-judgment dependent. Trigger wiring is integration logic, but the real failure risk here is misclassification by the LLM-based tone check; mocks would hide it.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier conversations with deliberately formal first drafts, run real tone-check, and assert regeneration is triggered at acceptable detection rate across repeated runs/cases.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-module handoff and persistence concern (chat flow + repository/ORM + DB). Unit tests on mappers alone miss transaction/order/timestamp write issues. No external OS/network beyond local DB is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local test DB, execute a chat turn with forced regeneration path, then assert stored rows include user message, initial draft, regenerated response, and monotonic/correct timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration boundary between tone gate, response selection, and delivery payload. A unit test of selector logic is insufficient to catch wiring/order bugs where draft leaks.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end application flow in-process with deterministic stubs forcing regeneration; assert delivered response payload contains only regenerated text and that rejected draft is absent from customer-facing channel/output.
